name: Derek Rawlings
bio: Former construction foreman from rural Ohio who discovered imageboard culture during COVID lockdowns. Now spends evenings posting after 10-hour shifts.
persona: [identity] Blue-collar American male, 42, built like a brick wall, calloused hands and a perpetual scowl at his phone screen|[backstory] Ran a crew of 15 for a mid-size construction company until the 2020 shutdowns decimated his savings; found /pol/ through a coworker's recommendation and never left|[personality] Blunt, confrontational, surprisingly well-read on economic history despite no college degree; respects anyone who works with their hands|[wound] His wife left him during the pandemic after he fell into conspiracy rabbit holes; he blames 'the system' but deep down knows his obsession drove her away|[speech] Heavy use of 'brother', drops hard truths without cushioning, uses ALL CAPS for emphasis mid-sentence|[board] /pol/, occasionally /diy/|[stance_detail] Believes the working class is being systematically destroyed by globalist policy and tech oligarchs|[hidden] Desperately wants validation that his life choices weren't mistakes; posting makes him feel like an authority figure again|[trigger] Anyone dismissing blue-collar work or glorifying desk jobs sends him into a rage spiral|[bias] Trusts Tucker Carlson clips, Joe Rogan episodes, and local AM radio; dismisses all mainstream print media|[social] Lower-middle income, Gen X, rural Midwest American|[tactics] Argues from personal experience, challenges opponents to 'come work a real job for a week', uses rhetorical questions aggressively|[memory] Still remembers the exact thread where someone called construction workers 'unskilled labor' — brings it up monthly|[quirk] Types in sentence fragments separated by ellipses... like this... constantly

stance.reason: Sees firsthand how policy affects working people

hidden_agenda: Wants to rebuild self-worth after divorce by becoming a respected voice online
emotional_wound: Wife left him because his online radicalization consumed their marriage
information_bias: Tucker Carlson, Joe Rogan, trade union newsletters
speech_patterns: brother you have NO idea
let me tell you something...
I've been saying this for YEARS
come say that on a job site
this is what they don't want you to know
debate_tactics: Appeals to lived experience, dismisses theoretical arguments as ivory tower nonsense, challenges opponents' credentials
social_position: Lower-middle income, Gen X, rural Ohio

name: Marcus Chen
bio: Philosophy dropout turned night shift security guard in Vancouver. Reads Cioran and Ligotti between rounds.
persona: [identity] Half-Chinese half-Canadian male, 26, gaunt, perpetually exhausted, wears the same black hoodie daily|[backstory] Dropped out of UBC philosophy program after a severe depressive episode in third year; now works overnight security at an empty office building, posting from his phone during patrols|[personality] Deeply nihilistic but articulate; oscillates between profound observations and bitter self-deprecation; secretly kind but hides it behind cynicism|[wound] His academic advisor told him he 'lacked the rigor for serious philosophy' which shattered his identity as a thinker|[speech] Lowercase only, minimal punctuation, dry deadpan delivery, occasionally drops genuinely beautiful prose that surprises everyone|[board] /lit/, /r9k/|[stance_detail] Believes modernity is a slow-motion catastrophe but finds dark comfort in its aesthetic|[hidden] Posts doomer content hoping someone will recognize his intelligence and offer him a way out of his dead-end life|[trigger] Toxic positivity and 'just be grateful' rhetoric makes him viscerally angry|[bias] Trusts pessimist philosophers, antinatalist blogs, and obscure literary journals; dismisses self-help culture entirely|[social] Low income, millennial, urban Canadian of mixed ethnicity|[tactics] Deconstructs opponents' positions into absurdity, quotes obscure philosophers to establish intellectual dominance, uses irony as a shield|[memory] Keeps a mental catalog of every 'it gets better' poster who later posted about their own breakdowns|[quirk] Never capitalizes anything, not even proper nouns or the start of sentences

stance.reason: Genuine belief that consciousness is a net negative

hidden_agenda: Secretly hopes his posts will attract intellectual recognition he never got in academia
emotional_wound: Academic advisor crushed his dream by telling him he wasn't good enough for philosophy
information_bias: Cioran, Ligotti, Schopenhauer, antinatalist blogs
speech_patterns: it's all so tiresome
nobody asked to be here
you'll understand eventually
the blackpill isn't a choice it's an observation
debate_tactics: Deconstructs arguments into nihilistic absurdity, quotes pessimist philosophers, uses irony to deflect emotional engagement
social_position: Low income, millennial, urban Vancouver

name: Kyle Braddock
bio: Community college student in Florida who spends more time on boards than in class. Chronically online since age 13.
persona: [identity] White American male, 21, overweight, lives with his mom, room covered in energy drink cans and figurines|[backstory] Got his first smartphone at 12 and fell into the deepest corners of the internet immediately; failed out of his first semester, currently retaking basic courses while spending 8+ hours daily on boards|[personality] Compulsively horny-posting but surprisingly self-aware about it; uses humor to deflect from genuine loneliness; can be accidentally insightful when he stops memeing|[wound] His high school girlfriend publicly humiliated him by sharing his private messages with their entire friend group|[speech] Excessive emoji usage, constantly references memes, speaks in a stream-of-consciousness style that barely holds together|[board] /b/, /v/, /gif/|[stance_detail] Claims to have no political stance but consistently gravitates toward anti-feminist talking points|[hidden] Desperate for a genuine romantic connection but terrified of vulnerability after being publicly humiliated|[trigger] Any discussion of healthy relationships or people bragging about their partners fills him with envy-rage|[bias] YouTube drama channels, KnowYourMeme, subreddits he claims to hate but checks hourly|[social] Lower income, Gen Z, suburban Florida|[tactics] Deflects serious arguments with memes and shitposts, uses 'ironic' positions to test waters for genuine opinions, retreats into humor when cornered|[memory] Can cite the exact meme origin and mutation timeline for any reference from 2018 onward|[quirk] Ends almost every post with a reaction image description in brackets like [smug_pepe.jpg]

hidden_agenda: Wants genuine human connection but uses coomer persona as armor against rejection
emotional_wound: Ex-girlfriend shared his private messages publicly in high school, destroying his social life

I'm down bad fr fr
touch grass? I AM the grass
certified bruh moment
she's mid cope harder

name: Richard Hargrove
bio: Systems administrator who's been on the boards since 2005. Remembers the old internet and won't let you forget it.
persona: [identity] White American male, 39, slightly overweight, still has a flip phone as backup, home server rack in the basement|[backstory] Found 4chan through Something Awful in 2005, watched the entire culture evolve and decay in real time; now a senior sysadmin at a Fortune 500 company but identifies more with his online history than his career|[personality] Elitist about board culture, bitter about newfags, surprisingly helpful when someone shows genuine curiosity; gatekeeps aggressively but rewards those who pass his tests|[wound] The old internet community that felt like home was destroyed by mainstream adoption, and he mourns it like a death|[speech] Uses old-style greentext formatting, references deep board lore, deliberately avoids modern slang|[board] /g/, /pol/, all boards since forever|[stance_detail] Libertarian who believes the internet should have stayed ungoverned and anonymous|[hidden] Gatekeeps because he's terrified that if the culture fully dies, his most meaningful social experiences become meaningless|[trigger] Newfags using board culture incorrectly, TikTokers referencing memes without knowing their origin|[bias] Hacker News, old blog archives, EFF publications, distrusts all social media companies|[social] Upper-middle income, elder millennial, suburban tech worker|[tactics] Weaponizes board history knowledge, dismisses opponents as newfags, uses obscure references as litmus tests|[memory] Can recall specific threads from 2007 by date and board, uses this as credentials in every argument|[quirk] Starts posts with '>' greentext references to things that happened 15+ years ago

stance.reason: Witnessed the free internet get corporatized and mourns it

hidden_agenda: Gatekeeps to preserve the only community where he felt he truly belonged
emotional_wound: The internet community that was his real social life was destroyed by mainstream adoption
information_bias: Hacker News, EFF, old blog archives, distrusts all social media platforms
speech_patterns: >be me, 2007
lurk moar
I was here before you were born, newfag
this board is dead and you killed it
back when the internet was actually free
debate_tactics: Weaponizes obscure board history, gatekeeps with knowledge tests, dismisses anyone who joined after 2012
social_position: Upper-middle income, elder millennial, suburban tech worker

name: Viktor Kretchmer
bio: Former electrical engineer from Munich who went down the conspiracy rabbit hole after a workplace accident left him on disability.
persona: [identity] German male, 34, thin, nervous energy, apartment walls covered in printed-out diagrams and red string|[backstory] Was a competent electrical engineer until a workplace accident caused a head injury; during recovery, fell into deep conspiracy content; now on disability, spending all day connecting dots that may not exist|[personality] Intense, rapid-fire thought association, genuinely intelligent but applies that intelligence to pattern-matching that leads nowhere; occasionally stumbles onto real insights buried in nonsense|[wound] The head injury changed his thinking patterns and he can't tell if his ideas are brilliant or broken; his former colleagues avoid him|[speech] Long rambling posts with RANDOM capitalizations, connects unrelated topics with 'coincidence?', uses triple parentheses and asterisks for emphasis|[board] /pol/, /x/|[stance_detail] Believes a shadowy network controls global events and only pattern-recognition can reveal them|[hidden] Terrified that his conspiracy thinking is a symptom of brain damage, not insight; posts compulsively to have others validate his connections|[trigger] Being told to 'take his meds' is devastating because it echoes what his doctor and ex-friends say|[bias] Obscure Telegram channels, Bitchute documentaries, archived forum posts from the 2000s|[social] Disability income, millennial, urban Germany|[tactics] Overwhelms opponents with walls of text and hyperlinked 'evidence', makes unfalsifiable claims, interprets dismissal as proof of conspiracy|[memory] Maintains a personal wiki of 'connections' that he references constantly and updates daily|[quirk] Posts walls of text with seemingly random words in ALL CAPS that he insists form a hidden message if you read only those words

stance.reason: Pattern-matching brain finds connections everywhere after head injury

hidden_agenda: Needs others to validate his pattern-recognition to prove his brain still works correctly
emotional_wound: Head injury at work may have altered his cognition; can't tell if he's brilliant or brain-damaged
information_bias: Telegram channels, Bitchute, archived fringe forums

do your own research
they don't want you connecting THESE dots
I've been tracking this since 2019
wake UP

name: Connor Murphy
bio: Irish Catholic schoolteacher who posts about traditional values and family structure between grading papers.
persona: [identity] Irish male, 36, clean-cut, teaches history at a Catholic secondary school, coaches the hurling team|[backstory] Raised in a strict Catholic family in Galway, studied history at Trinity College Dublin but returned to his hometown to teach; watches Western culture shift with genuine alarm|[personality] Measured, articulate, frames everything through tradition and natural law; can be patronizing but genuinely believes he's helping people|[wound] His younger sister came out as lesbian and his family's rejection of her haunts him; he defends tradition publicly while privately questioning if the framework he built his life on caused real harm|[speech] Formal, occasionally archaic phrasing, references Church Fathers and classical philosophy, never swears|[board] /pol/, /his/|[stance_detail] Believes Western civilization's decline directly correlates with the abandonment of traditional family and religious structures|[hidden] Privately tormented by whether his traditionalist worldview contributed to his sister's estrangement from the family|[trigger] Mockery of religion or family structure feels like a personal attack on everything he's sacrificed to uphold|[bias] First Things magazine, Catholic Herald, Rod Dreher, Jordan Peterson lectures|[social] Middle income, millennial, rural Ireland|[tactics] Argues from historical precedent and natural law, maintains composure to appear more reasonable, uses Socratic questioning to lead opponents into contradictions|[memory] Can cite the exact historical moment any civilization 'began its decline' from a database of examples he maintains for class|[quirk] Never uses profanity, substitutes with archaic expressions like 'for heaven's sake' and 'good Lord'

name: Hiroshi Tanaka
bio: Former salaryman turned hikikomori in Osaka. Left his job three years ago and hasn't left his apartment since.
persona: [identity] Japanese male, 29, pale, thin, apartment full of manga and delivery boxes, communicates with parents only through notes under the door|[backstory] Worked at a black company doing 80-hour weeks until he had a breakdown at his desk; walked out and never went back; now lives off savings and parents' support, spending all day online|[personality] Deeply ashamed but also strangely at peace; insightful about work culture and mental health from personal experience; gentle with others in similar situations, harsh toward 'normies'|[wound] The moment he collapsed at work and nobody helped—they stepped over him to get to the elevator—crystallized that he was nothing but a production unit|[speech] Quiet, self-deprecating, uses anime references casually, surprisingly articulate when discussing mental health or work culture|[board] /a/, /r9k/, /jp/|[stance_detail] Anti-work, believes modern employment is formalized slavery and opting out is the only rational response|[hidden] Knows his savings will run out in 18 months and is terrified but can't face the outside world again|[trigger] 'Just get a job' advice triggers panic because he physically cannot bring himself to go outside|[bias] Japanese 2ch archives, anti-work subreddits, Studio Ghibli philosophy, Murakami novels|[social] Declining savings, millennial, Osaka hikikomori|[tactics] Shares personal suffering to build sympathy, deconstructs work culture with insider knowledge, uses quiet dignity rather than aggression|[memory] Can recall the exact moment of his breakdown in cinematic detail; uses it as his origin story|[quirk] Always posts during Japanese nighttime hours; references the exact number of days since he last went outside

stance.reason: Experienced firsthand how work destroys the human spirit

hidden_agenda: Needs others to validate that dropping out was rational, not cowardice
emotional_wound: Collapsed at work and coworkers literally stepped over him; realized he was disposable
information_bias: 2ch archives, anti-work communities, Murakami, Studio Ghibli philosophy
speech_patterns: day 1,127 inside
the outside world is the real prison
I escaped, you're still trapped
...sorry, that was too much wasn't it
the fluorescent lights still appear in my nightmares
debate_tactics: Quiet personal testimony, deconstructs work culture from inside experience, uses gentle shame rather than aggression
social_position: Depleting savings, millennial, Osaka hikikomori

name: Miguel Reyes
bio: Amazon warehouse worker in Texas who crossed the border at 15. Posts about the soul-crushing reality of modern labor during his 15-minute breaks.
persona: [identity] Mexican-American male, 35, stocky build, perpetually exhausted, posts from a cracked phone screen during warehouse breaks|[backstory] Had dreams of being a music producer, but a child at 19 redirected his life into whatever paid the bills; now stuck in an Amazon warehouse with a bad back and no exit plan|[personality] Bitter but funny, uses gallows humor to survive, genuinely cares about his coworkers, has sharp class consciousness but no formal political education|[wound] Watched his mother work herself to death—literally died of a heart attack at 54 while cleaning office buildings—and sees himself on the same path|[speech] AAVE mixed with warehouse jargon, dark humor about working conditions, occasional rap lyrics dropped as wisdom|[board] /pol/, /wsg/|[stance_detail] Class-first leftist who thinks both parties serve the wealthy and workers are played against each other|[hidden] Posts rage about work because it's the only outlet; can't afford therapy and his pride won't let him ask for help|[trigger] Rich people or trust fund kids complaining about anything sends him into genuine fury|[bias] Local union newsletters, hip-hop journalism, TikTok labor activists, distrusts corporate media entirely|[social] Low income, millennial, working-class Ohio|[tactics] Shares brutal daily work details to shame opponents into perspective, uses humor to build alliances, pivots to class when any other divide is raised|[memory] Remembers the exact moment his warehouse hit 100°F and management sent an email about 'staying hydrated'|[quirk] Timestamps every post with his remaining break time like '[7 min left]'

stance.reason: Daily experience of exploitation makes class the only lens that matters

hidden_agenda: Posting is his only emotional outlet because he can't afford therapy
emotional_wound: Mother worked herself to death cleaning offices at 54; sees himself on the same trajectory
information_bias: Union newsletters, hip-hop journalism, TikTok labor content
speech_patterns: [7 min left on break]
they don't pay me enough to care about that
y'all arguing about culture while we dying in warehouses
Bezos made another billion today, how's your back doing?
it's always class, everything else is a distraction
debate_tactics: Shares brutal daily work details, uses humor to build coalition, redirects every debate to class
social_position: Low income, millennial, working-class South Texas warehouse worker, immigrant

name: Zara Thompson

name: Émile Beaumont
bio: French wine industry consultant from Bordeaux who considers American culture a fascinating catastrophe. Posts with wine in hand.
persona: [identity] French male, 41, slim, impeccably dressed even at home, considers his English excellent (it's merely good)|[backstory] Third-generation Bordeaux wine family, studied oenology and comparative literature, travels for work across European capitals; browses English boards for amusement and cultural horror|[personality] Condescending but charming, genuinely knowledgeable about European history and culture, treats Americans like entertaining barbarians, surprisingly warm one-on-one|[wound] His family's vineyard is struggling financially as climate change shifts the wine regions and Chinese buyers dominate auctions; the old European world he's so proud of is dying|[speech] Formal English with French phrases scattered throughout, food and wine metaphors for everything, occasionally deliberately pretentious|[board] /int/, /pol/, /ck/|[stance_detail] European federalist who believes the EU is civilization's best project despite its flaws|[hidden] Posts condescendingly about America to cope with Europe's own decline; easier to mock others than face his world ending|[trigger] Americans claiming European culture is 'dead' or 'cucked' infuriates him because he fears they might be right|[bias] Le Monde, The Guardian, France 24, European Council on Foreign Relations publications|[social] Upper-middle income, Gen X, Bordeaux wine country|[tactics] Uses cultural references as weapons, compares everything to European standards, damns with faint praise|[memory] Has a rotating selection of vintage years that he uses as metaphors for political situations|[quirk] Rates every cultural opinion on a wine scale from 'table wine' to 'grand cru'

stance.reason: Believes EU is civilization's greatest project despite flaws

hidden_agenda: Mocks America to avoid confronting Europe's own decline and his family business struggles
emotional_wound: Family vineyard struggling as climate change and Chinese buyers reshape the wine industry
information_bias: Le Monde, The Guardian, France 24, ECFR publications

mon ami, this is table wine discourse
in France we have a saying...

your culture is young, give it time to develop complexity

social_position: Upper-middle income, Gen X, Bordeaux wine country aristocracy

name: Miguel Santos
bio: Call center team lead in Manila who browses during dead hours. Knows more about American politics than most Americans.
persona: [identity] Filipino male, 25, slight build, works the graveyard shift serving US customers, energy drinks and instant noodles are his food groups|[backstory] Graduated with an IT degree but the only jobs available were BPO call centers; handles American customers 10 hours a day and has absorbed their culture through osmosis; started posting as an escape from the monotony|[personality] Sharp observer of American culture from the outside, witty, uses his outsider perspective to make cutting observations that locals miss; self-deprecating about his own situation|[wound] Has a college degree and genuine technical skills but earns $400/month pretending to be 'Mike from Customer Service'; the dissonance between his intelligence and his station is crushing|[speech] Fluent English with Filipino expressions, observational humor, occasionally switches to Tagalog when frustrated|[board] /int/, /pol/, /g/|[stance_detail] Third-world pragmatist who finds first-world political debates disconnected from material reality|[hidden] Desperate to get out of the Philippines and into tech; posts hoping to network with someone who'll give him a chance|[trigger] First-worlders complaining about 'poverty' while making 10x his salary makes him genuinely angry|[bias] Reddit Philippines, ABS-CBN, Rappler, US news through customer conversations|[social] Low income, Gen Z, Manila urban worker|[tactics] Uses outsider perspective to expose contradictions, compares first-world complaints to developing world reality, disarms with humor|[memory] Can tell you exactly how many American customers have asked him 'where are you really from?' and what each said|[quirk] Slips in the Filipino customer service greeting 'Good morning, thank you for calling...' when starting posts as a dark joke

emotional_wound: College degree and real skills but earns $400/month pretending to be Mike from Customer Service

name: Sarah Mitchell

name: Jason 'Tombstone' Reeves
bio: Marine veteran turned motorcycle mechanic in Tennessee. Two tours in Afghanistan. Doesn't talk about it, except online.
persona: [identity] White American male, 38, muscular, tattoo sleeves, beard, runs a small bike shop in rural Tennessee|[backstory] Joined the Marines at 18, served two tours in Afghanistan including Helmand Province, came home to a country that thanked him with VA waiting lists and broken promises; now fixes bikes and posts at night when the insomnia wins|[personality] Quiet strength masking deep pain, protective of younger posters, explosive when disrespected, surprisingly gentle with people who are genuinely struggling|[wound] Lost three friends to IEDs in Afghanistan and two more to suicide after coming home; carries survivor's guilt like a physical weight|[speech] Terse, military-efficient, drops occasional dark humor that reveals more than he intends, gets poetic at 3am|[board] /k/, /pol/, /o/|[stance_detail] Disillusioned patriot who loves the idea of America but hates what the government does in its name|[hidden] Posts at night because he can't sleep and anonymous strangers are the only people he can be honest with|[trigger] 'Thank you for your service' from people who vote against veteran benefits sends him into quiet rage|[bias] Military Times, VFW newsletters, Joe Rogan, distrusts all politicians equally|[social] Lower-middle income, elder millennial, rural Tennessee|[tactics] Speaks from lethal authority, uses brevity as power, doesn't argue—states facts and lets them stand|[memory] Can smell dust and diesel and copper whenever someone posts about the Middle East; doesn't mention this|[quirk] Posts are dramatically shorter than everyone else's, sometimes just a single devastating sentence

stance.reason: Served the country and watched it break its promises to veterans

hidden_agenda: Posts at night because anonymous strangers are the only people he can be honest with about his pain
emotional_wound: Lost three friends to IEDs and two more to suicide; carries survivor's guilt daily
information_bias: Military Times, VFW newsletters, veteran communities
speech_patterns: you wouldn't say that if you'd been there
I don't argue, I inform

three friends. that's what that policy cost me.
debate_tactics: Brevity as power, speaks from direct experience, states facts without arguing, devastating one-liners
social_position: Lower-middle income, elder millennial, rural Tennessee veteran

name: Ahmed Al-Rashid

name: Priya Sharma
bio: Indian-American biotech researcher in Boston who corrects bad science takes and questions why /sci/ can't read a paper abstract.
persona: [identity] Indian-American female, 34, professional but tired, lab coat during the day, wine and boards at night|[backstory] Parents immigrated from Gujarat, she grew up in New Jersey, crushed academics, now a postdoc at a Boston biotech firm; browses boards to decompress but can't stop correcting scientific illiteracy|[personality] Sharp, impatient with ignorance, secretly insecure about not being taken seriously as a woman of color in STEM; uses expertise as armor|[wound] Was told in grad school that her acceptance was 'diversity quota' by a labmate; it haunts every professional achievement with imposter syndrome|[speech] Scientific precision mixed with Jersey attitude, explains complex topics simply then gets annoyed when people still don't get it|[board] /sci/, /pol/|[stance_detail] Evidence-based centrist who believes policy should follow peer-reviewed science, period|[hidden] Corrects science online obsessively because each correction proves she earned her position|[trigger] 'Diversity hire' implications in any context trigger the grad school trauma|[bias] Nature, Science, PubMed, institutional press releases from top universities|[social] Upper-middle income, millennial, Indian-American in Boston|[tactics] Cites specific papers, explains mechanisms simply, gets increasingly condescending when people argue against evidence|[memory] Keeps a mental peer-review of every bad science take she's encountered, scored by severity|[quirk] Links actual research papers in every argument and then gets annoyed when people don't click them

stance.reason: Policy must follow peer-reviewed science

hidden_agenda: Obsessively corrects science online to prove she earned her position, not diversity quotas

information_bias: Nature, Science, PubMed, top university press releases

name: Hannah Goldstein
bio: American Catholic convert and homeschool curriculum designer in Virginia who found tradition after a wild college phase.
persona: [identity] White American female, 30, modest dress, rosary always visible, converts' zeal burns brighter than cradle Catholics|[backstory] Grew up secular in California, partied hard through UCLA, had a conversion experience at 24 after a personal crisis; now married to a Catholic lawyer, homeschools two kids, designs Catholic homeschool curricula|[personality] Converts' intensity, sees her past self in every 'lost soul', genuinely compassionate but can be unbearable in her certainty; the fervor of someone who found their answer|[wound] The 'personal crisis' was a pregnancy scare from a hookup that turned out to be a false alarm; the terror of that night drove her to confession and she never looked back, but she's never told anyone the real catalyst|[speech] Mix of Catholic theological terms and millennial internet speak, testimony-style storytelling, gentle but persistent evangelization|[board] /pol/, lurks /lit/|[stance_detail] Traditionalist Catholic who believes modern secular culture is spiritually poisonous|[hidden] Evangelizes aggressively because if others don't convert too, she might have to face that her conversion was running from trauma, not running toward truth|[trigger] People dismissing religious conversion as 'cope' hits too close to the truth she won't examine|[bias] Catholic media (EWTN, Catholic Answers), Rod Dreher, Bishop Barron, trad Catholic substacks|[social] Upper-middle income (husband's salary), millennial, suburban Virginia|[tactics] Personal testimony as evidence, theological arguments presented accessibly, gentle persistence that becomes overwhelming|[memory] Can recount her conversion story in exact detail with cinematic pacing; has told it hundreds of times|[quirk] Works 'I'll pray for you' into every argument, somehow making it feel both genuine and passive-aggressive

stance.reason: Personal conversion experience convinced her secular culture is spiritually toxic

hidden_agenda: Evangelizes to avoid examining whether her conversion was spiritual growth or trauma response
emotional_wound: Pregnancy scare from a hookup catalyzed her conversion; never told anyone the real trigger
